5469d3e92c1d.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
5469d3e92c1d.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
5469d3e92c1d.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

88f503e66194.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
88f503e66194.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

602ed816780a.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
602ed816780a.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
602ed816780a.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

32bad3e812f8.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
32bad3e812f8.description: Your 5/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P25.0). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
32bad3e812f8.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

95871d383e30.title: Ranked #7 of 9 locally — peers are ahead
95871d383e30.description: At percentile 25.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

9838264d92fb.title: Accelerate review volume — you're credible but not dominant
9838264d92fb.description: At 26 reviews, your rating is stable but not commanding. Venues with 200+ reviews rank higher in Maps. A post-visit review prompt would compound monthly.
